content:
The trade was initiated using an order block, based on the premise of a market maker buy model. The
objective is to send price up into the new week opening gap, which was mentioned earlier on Twitter.

A shaded area on the chart might be perceived as a breaker by some, but it is not. It is simply a speed
bump, and price is expected to move through it. Many traders using a 'rip-off edition' of smart money
concepts would incorrectly identify this as a sell area.

section_title: DRAW ON LIQUIDITY AND DOWN CLOSED CANDLE ENTRY
content:
The market is drawn to specific buy side liquidity. There's another order block present. My assessment is
that price will drop to a particular level, digging down into a down closed candle. The trade can move down
to the midpoint of that candle, which is the mean threshold. This is considered acceptable price action.

section_title: ADDING CONTRACTS AND TARGETING HIGH OF THE DAY
content:
Five additional contracts were added to the position. The expectation is for price to vault quickly to take out
the high of the day, as noted on the chart. The chart's shading was adjusted for better visibility.

section_title: EXPECTATION OF EXPANSION AND ON-SIDE CONFIRMATION
content:
The trade is looking for aggressive expansion and speed. The stop loss was moved to break even,
confirming the position is 'on-side,' meaning the initial directional bias is most likely correct. Price should
aggressively trade to the buy side and then move into the new week opening gap, which is represented by
a shaded orange level.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF TWITTER CONTEXT AND SENSITIVE PRICE POINTS
content:
Without following the prior commentary on Twitter, the rationale for current price action might not be clear.
The most recent down closed candle, from its high to its open, represents a very sensitive price point. No
further additions to the position are planned at this juncture.

section_title: REACTION TO ORDER BLOCK AND NEW WEEK OPENING GAP DEFINITION
content:
Price reacted as expected off the order block and then ripped into the buy side liquidity. The current plan is
proceeding successfully. The target is 4076.75, which is the low end of the new week opening gap. The
new week opening gap is defined as the difference between Friday's close and the very first tick of Sunday
evening's opening price.

section_title: EXPANSION, FAIR VALUE GAPS, AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT
content:
Price is expected to expand into the shaded area. After tapping into buy side liquidity, a pullback is
possible. A small fair value gap just formed if the current candle closes. Consequent encroachment, which
is being measured, is used to manage the position. Eighty percent of the position (20 contracts) will be
taken off.

section_title: TARGETING THE NEW WEEK OPENING GAP EXTREMES
content:
After taking eighty percent of the position off, the thought process shifts to targeting the top end of the new
week opening gap, represented by the high end of the shaded orange area. However, the immediate focus
remains on reaching the bottom of the gap, specifically the 'draw on liquidity' level.

section_title: TRADE EXECUTION DETAILS AND ENTRY CONFIRMATION
content:
The highest point formed around 3:20. Short-term highs were broken to the upside. The initial buy entry
was inside the first down closed candle as price was moving lower, annotating it as an order block. Price
later touched this order block again, but no additional contracts were added. The trade is now within
striking distance of the new week opening gap.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING NARRATIVE AND AVOIDING MISINTERPRETATION OF PD ARRAYS
content:
Understanding the narrative of price action is crucial to avoid misinterpreting PD Arrays. Without proper
understanding, concepts can be misused by those pretending to be experts, leading to harmful outcomes
through cherry-picking and hindsight analysis.

section_title: HITTING THE NEW WEEK OPENING GAP AND EXPECTED REACTIONS
content:
Price has now hit the new week opening gap. A pullback into the fair value gap just below is possible. If it
drops into that, an immediate rejection and continuation higher is desired. A shaded pink area, which some
might misinterpret as a bearish breaker, is not; it is a long opportunity. It is imperative to understand the
underlying concepts to avoid self-harm or misleading others.

Now that the bottom of the new week opening gap has been hit, the focus shifts to the consequent
encroachment (CE). Consequent encroachment is defined as the midpoint of any inefficiency or gap. It is
abbreviated as C.E. in charts and annotations. Price is expected to find momentum and spring out of the
fair value gap towards the CE.

section_title: PEELING OFF CONTRACTS AND STOP MANAGEMENT
content:
Twenty contracts will be peeled off as soon as the consequent encroachment level is touched. After this,
the stop loss will be rolled up to lock in profit (2000 units). The remaining position requires support without
re-entering the lower quarter of the consequent encroachment to the low of the shaded area.
Reaccumulation and expansion higher are desired. The stop is intentionally close due to tomorrow's
FOMC, where price can become volatile, making it unwise to allow a full retracement into the lower fair
value gap.

The stop placement is strategic; if it's hit, the outcome is acceptable. The new focus is to reach the top of
the new week opening gap, which is the shaded area. Eighty percent of the position has already been
taken off. The contract selection will be dropped to one contract to start peeling off singles.

section_title: MANAGING REMAINING CONTRACTS IN THE UPPER HALF OF NWOG
content:
As price digs into the upper half of the new week opening gap, attention is on relative equal highs forming
on the one-minute chart. This process is called 'running down equity.' When a new candle prints just above
these relative equal highs by one tick, one contract will be immediately taken off. This gradual reduction is
important in a prolonged delivery move.

section_title: IDENTIFYING 'BULL FLAGS' AND PATIENCE FOR ENTRIES
content:
This represents a 'real bull flag' scenario. An opportunity to peel off a contract was missed, requiring
patience to wait for the next setup. Confidence remains that price will run to the top end of the new week
opening gap. The strategy is to watch for a higher high printing on the current candle; once it does, one
contract will be sold at market from the remaining five.

section_title: SEQUENTIAL PARTIAL EXITS AND FINAL STOP MANAGEMENT
content:
The next target for peeling off a contract is approximately halfway between the current price and the top of
the shaded area. After this, only three contracts remain. Another pop-up is anticipated for the next partial
exit. For new traders, this can be nerve-wracking. Once another contract is peeled off, leaving the final
balance, the stop loss will be rolled up. If it triggers, it is an acceptable outcome given the beautiful price
run.

The stop was eventually hit. The executions will be shown, confirming they are not from a market replay.
Hope was expressed that the viewer found value in the session. Be safe until next time.
